target: all fabrication files identify revision r12
method: Compare CAD, DXF, drawing, and README revision IDs.
notes: Block fabrication if any source file is stale or unnamed.

target: units, scale, origin, and stock zero are explicit
tolerance: known dimension within 0.005 in or 0.1 mm
method: Open DXF/CAD in CAM and verify a known dimension plus work origin.
notes: Do not infer inches versus millimeters from visual scale.

target: 100 percent of layers have operation intent
method: Map every DXF layer/color to cut, engrave, pocket, drill, or ignore.
notes: Unmapped geometry must be resolved before toolpath export.

target: stock matches design callouts and fits selected machine
method: Check stock size, thickness, flatness, grain/face orientation, and machine bed clearance.
notes: Record substitutions as a new revision or explicit waiver.

target: fixture does not conflict with cutting envelope
tolerance: clearance exceeds tool radius plus safety margin
method: Verify clamps, tape, tabs, vacuum zones, screws, and toolpath clearances.
notes: Include hold-down locations in the repo or setup sheet.

method: Review tool library, feeds/speeds, cut depth, tabs, kerf compensation, and postprocessor.
notes: CAM settings belong in the fabrication repo when they drive repeatability.

target: material and operation are allowed by the shop
method: Check posted shop rules, material policy, dust/fume controls, PPE, and emergency stop path.
notes: Laser plastics and unknown coatings require material verification.

target: dry run clear and first article within tolerance
method: Air cut or frame trace, then inspect the first article against critical dimensions.
notes: Do not batch-run until the first article passes.
